_readme: Copy source for the About page + dialog. Edit freely — the renderer picks up changes on next HMR / rebuild. Sections marked `_status: "draft"` still need owner review. When a section is finalized, change status to `final`.

tagline.text: Local-first eBook intelligence for researchers and academics.

paragraphs: ShortCut Studio turns a messy drawer of PDFs, EPUBs, and papers into a navigable library organised by topic and super-category. It scans your drives, runs classification using the LLM you choose (local or remote), and keeps the results orderly — without uploading anything to the cloud unless you explicitly say so.
It's built for researchers who accumulate thousands of documents and want to see across them by concept, not by filename.

mission.heading: Make deep libraries navigable.
mission.body: Research papers, technical books, and long-form content should be searchable the way your brain thinks — by idea, not by filename. ShortCut Studio brings that shape to a folder of PDFs.

vision.heading: A peer-powered research OS.
vision.body: A future where every document is classified once across a trusted peer network, so nobody pays the extraction cost twice. Private work stays yours; shared-by-default public work benefits everyone.

steps.title: Pick folders to scan
steps.detail: Tell ShortCut where your eBooks and research papers live.

steps.title: Configure an LLM
steps.detail: Use Ollama for fully-local inference, or add an OpenAI / Claude / Gemini key.

steps.title: Review topics as they generate
steps.detail: Approve, rename, or group AI-suggested topics into super-categories.

steps.title: Monitor progress
steps.detail: The Progress Glass shows lifetime progress plus the delta from your chosen time range.

steps.title: Keep sensitive work private
steps.detail: Add private terms so matching files route to the Private library and never leave your machine.

privacy.headline: Privacy-first by default.
privacy.body: Commercial LLM providers may learn from what you feed them. ShortCut Studio defaults to local-first inference via Ollama, and never uploads a file's contents without your explicit choice of provider.
facts: Stays on this machine — your eBooks, PDFs, and the SQLite databases never leave your drives.
Only sent to an LLM when you trigger classification — and only to the provider you picked.
The peer network (when enabled) shares document hashes for deduplication, never file contents.

paragraphs: When you dedicate disk and CPU to the peer network, each paper in your library gets classified once — globally. If a peer already processed a document, you get the result instantly; if not, your machine might be the one that helps someone else.
This is opt-in. The peer network UI works in this build, but the backend that coordinates it (ExecEngine) is still in development — so for now your local processing is all there is.
community.statusNote: Peer network UI is live; the coordinating backend is in development. Expect real peer data in a future release.

paragraphs: Built by a small team with researchers as the primary audience. The backend infrastructure is maintained by the ExecEngine project; worker binaries come from SCL_Demo; this desktop client is ShortCut Studio.
credits.acknowledgment: Built on the shoulders of many open-source projects — thank you to the Electron, React, Vite, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, Radix UI, and SQLite communities.

feedback.note: Feedback, bug reports, and feature requests — contact channel TBD.
